title: Imagine Cup World Championship
sortTitle: imagine cup world championship
description: The final moment is here. Three startups. One stage. This year’s finalists take the stage pitching their solutions as they compete to become the 2026 Imagine Cup World Champion, win $100,000 USD, and a mentorship session with Microsoft Chairman and CEO, Satya Nadella. This is where ideas meet execution and where student founders show what it looks like to build with scale in mind.

title: Scott and Mark learn to Vibe Check
sortTitle: scott and mark learn to vibe check
description: AI can turn an idea into a working demo faster than ever. But can that demo survive two experts who have seen every trick in the book? In this live Build showcase, developers present AI-assisted apps, agents, tools, and workflows to Mark Russinovich and Scott Hanselman. Mark and Scott will ask how it works, where the seams are, what the AI actually built, and whether the result is clever prototype, production-ready software, or something unexpectedly magical. Come for the demos. Stay for the technical reveal.

title: Agent supervision is the new senior engineering skill
sortTitle: agent supervision is the new senior engineering skill
description:
As AI agents start shipping code, the bottleneck becomes trust. This talk argues “agent supervision” is the new senior skill: scoping agent work, setting constraints, designing checkpoints, and reviewing outputs for correctness and security. Learn practical patterns to catch silent failures and build workflows where humans stay accountable and agents stay fast.


Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: AI & Agent-Augmented coding you can trust on Windows
sortTitle: ai & agent-augmented coding you can trust on windows
description:
Agents now run real commands, modify files, and move data. This breakout highlights the full set of Windows primitives that help agents discover, reason about, and execute AI‑powered activities across the system, while still operating within clear boundaries. Through live demos, you’ll see how agents identify available capabilities, plan and take meaningful actions, and how Windows layers permission scoping, inspection, developer tool capabilities, and rollback to keep developers in control.

title: Build AI across client and cloud with AMD ROCm and Microsoft
sortTitle: build ai across client and cloud with amd rocm and microsoft
description:
As AI moves from experimentation to production, developers need practical ways to build, test, and optimize workloads across client, cloud, and on-prem environments. Learn how AMD ROCm™ provides a common AI software foundation across Radeon™, Ryzen™ AI, and AMD Instinct™ platforms. title: Build secure and enterprise-ready agents with Agent 365
sortTitle: build secure and enterprise-ready agents with agent 365
description:
As AI agents move to production, developers need observability, governance, and security across the agent lifecycle. We'll show how to build enterprise-ready agents using Agent 365 SDK and Purview SDK to instrument runtime visibility, enforce identity-aware access, protect data, and apply policy-based governance. Through practical examples, you'll learn to meet security and compliance requirements without slowing development. Ship agents that are powerful and trustworthy by default.

sortTitle: claw and agent harness in microsoft foundry

title: Deploy. Observe. Learn. Reinforcement learning for production agents
sortTitle: deploy. observe. learn. reinforcement learning for production agents
description:
Agents don't fail in demos — they fail in production. In this breakout, see how teams use fine-tuning and reinforcement learning on Microsoft Foundry to improve production agents using real usage signals. We cover when fine-tuning reduces cost and latency, when RL delivers deeper gains, and how Foundry makes it easy to train, evaluate, and redeploy safely. Johnson & Johnson shares their journey from RL experiments to production.

title: Distributed systems to AI platforms with Mark Russinovich & Ion Stoica
sortTitle: distributed systems to ai platforms with mark russinovich & ion stoica
description:
What will it take to build AI platforms for the agent era? Join Azure CTO Mark Russinovich and UC Berkeley professor Ion Stoica (co-creator of Apache Spark and co-founder of Databricks and Anyscale) to explore how AI infrastructure must evolve as systems become agentic, multimodal, and globally distributed. Get practical insights on next generation architectures, from training to real time serving, and why open source, security, and governance are now core platform concerns.

title: From CLI to PR: Automating the path to merged code
sortTitle: from cli to pr: automating the path to merged code
description:
Everyone talks about agents, but the real challenge is applying them to daily sprints. Moving beyond chat, we'll show how GitHub Copilot functions as an agentic partner in your workflow by live-coding a full cycle—from planning in the terminal to delegating work to the cloud and automating PR reviews. No high-level abstractions here. Just technical mechanics: context management, advanced features with Copilot CLI, and the patterns that make agentic workflows actually stick.

speakerNames: Vivek Bhadauria, Sebastian Kohlmeier, Filisha Shah
title: From observability to ROI for AI agents on any framework
sortTitle: from observability to roi for ai agents on any framework
description:
Nondeterministic, multi-agent systems break traditional monitoring. As agents reach production, observability must be built in—not added after failures. This session covers modern agent observability: cross-framework tracing and evals, rigorous inner-loop practices, evolving context-specific evals, and always-on signals that connect behavior to business outcomes to measure value, cost, and ROI.

title: Stop routing docstrings to 70B models with on-device AI on Snapdragon
sortTitle: stop routing docstrings to 70b models with on-device ai on snapdragon
description:
Your AI coding assistant calls a 70B+ cloud model just to add a docstring. Snapdragon X2 Elite’s 80 TOPS NPU changes that. In this session, build a three-tier inference routing architecture—on‑device (≤13B), on‑prem (14B–34B), and cloud (70B+)—cutting cloud tokens by 67%, latency by 70%, and keeping most code local. Includes routing logic, quantization trade‑offs, and a deployable classifier.

speakerNames: Harmke Alkemade, Dimitri Maltezakis, Anton Slutsky
title: Build and deploy reasoning agents with NVIDIA Nemotron and Foundry
sortTitle: build and deploy reasoning agents with nvidia nemotron and foundry
description:
Build and deploy intelligent AI agents using NVIDIA AI-Q Blueprint and Nemotron with Microsoft Foundry, Azure Container Apps, and Azure AI Search. In this hands-on lab, you'll create an agent for enterprise deep research that combines AI reasoning, multimodal data extraction, and retrieval-augmented generation to synthesize data into actionable insights. Learn how to connect data sources and run inference at scale—then apply these patterns to your own production workflows.

This Lab is BYOD. Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

title: Create advanced Postgres-powered agentic apps with Azure HorizonDB
sortTitle: create advanced postgres-powered agentic apps with azure horizondb
description:
In this lab, you’ll build a production-ready AI agent using Azure HorizonDB as a unified data, retrieval, and intelligence layer. Implement RAG, hybrid semantic and keyword search, and agentic workflows without stitching external services. Use DiskANN advanced filtering and in-database AI model management to run LLMs and embeddings directly in the database - no vector stores or glue code. Ideal for developers and architects who want simpler stacks, higher performance, and faster AI delivery.

Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

title: From data to context: Agent‑ready knowledge with Foundry IQ
sortTitle: from data to context: agent‑ready knowledge with foundry iq
description:
What if your coding agent could reason over enterprise knowledge—across files, data, and work signals—without leaving your developer workflow? In this hands-on lab, you’ll build a reusable knowledge base in Foundry IQ using the Microsoft Foundry portal, expose it as an MCP server, and connect it to GitHub Copilot CLI—no custom RAG plumbing required. Experience how Foundry IQ and Copilot CLI work together to turn enterprise knowledge into live context, right where developers build and debug.

Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

title: From zero to deployed on Azure with AI agents
sortTitle: from zero to deployed on azure with ai agents
description:
What happens when you let AI agents do the building? In this hands-on lab, you'll go from an empty terminal to a deployed app on Azure — with GitHub Copilot CLI and coding agents handling the scaffolding, coding, debugging, and deployment. You'll use the new Azure skills to provision resources and wire up services through natural language, no portal required. This isn't a demo you watch. You'll walk out with a real, working dev workflow you can take straight to your next project.

Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

title: Generate video with PyTorch and ComfyUI: Build a GPU pipeline
sortTitle: generate video with pytorch and comfyui: build a gpu pipeline
description:
Build a video generation pipeline using open-source models and GPU acceleration. In this hands-on lab, you’ll use PyTorch and ComfyUI to generate video clips, configure workflows, and optimize performance. Learn how to run and customize video generation pipelines, reduce compute costs, and apply these techniques to real AI scenarios. Use your skills to participate in a challenge to win prizes.

This Lab is BYOD. Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

title: Get Started with Models in Microsoft Foundry to Build AI Apps
sortTitle: get started with models in microsoft foundry to build ai apps
description:
In this hands-on lab, you will build a production-ready AI application using Microsoft Foundry, with no fine-tuning or deep machine learning expertise required. You will discover and select models, provision a Foundry project, and connect to a hosted model using the OpenAI SDK. You’ll implement a comment moderation workflow, compare model outputs, and package the solution as a hosted agent using Python, ready for real-world integration.

Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

speakerNames: Gova Babu, Jamshed Damkewala, Amar Dhamdhere
title: Hands-on with the latest Cobalt VMs – from code to cloud deployment
sortTitle: hands-on with the latest cobalt vms – from code to cloud deployment
description:
Roll up your sleeves and get hands-on with the latest Azure Cobalt VMs. Provision your first Cobalt VM and explore what makes Arm tick in the Cloud. From there you'll build multi-arch container images with Docker, push them to ACR, and deploy to AKS clusters running on Cobalt VMs. Next, wire up a CI/CD pipeline that builds, tests, and deploys to Arm. Then go further—deploy a full-stack web app and serve AI predictions using ONNX Runtime on Arm. Walk out ready to build on Cobalt VMs.

Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

title: Improving agent behavior using reinforcement learning from traces
sortTitle: improving agent behavior using reinforcement learning from traces
description:
AI agents often work, but their behavior can be inconsistent or hard to control with prompts alone. In this hands-on lab, you will start with a working agent, inspect execution traces, define “good behavior” using evaluation graders, and apply reinforcement learning to reinforce better decisions. By the end, you’ll have a practical workflow for improving agent behavior using feedback from agent runs, without redesigning your agent from scratch.

Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

By the end, you’ll have a practical workflow for improving agent behavior using feedback from agent runs, without redesigning your agent from scratch.

Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

title: Make GitHub Copilot Work Your Way: Custom Tools, Context and Workflows
sortTitle: make github copilot work your way: custom tools, context and workflows
description:
GitHub Copilot is powerful out of the box, but custom agents let you shape it to fit your team's exact workflows. In this lab, you'll build agents from scratch: set their roles, write instructions, scope their tools, and plug them into real workflows. You'll create reusable Agent Skills for domain knowledge and connect agents to external services via MCP. Everything works across VS Code, Visual Studio, CLI, and Copilot coding agent. Leave with working agents you can adapt to your own codebase.

title: Observe, optimize and protect your hosted agents in Microsoft Foundry
sortTitle: observe, optimize and protect your hosted agents in microsoft foundry
description:
Modern agents fail in ways traditional monitoring can’t catch. In this hands-on lab, learn how Microsoft Foundry Observability helps you move from prototype → production with context-specific evaluation suites (auto-generated evaluators + test datasets) wired into developer workflows via skills/MCP tooling for hosted agents. Then scale quality with continuous evaluation, trace-linked analysis, and adaptive red teaming—and walk away with a sandbox to explore additional features on your own.

Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

title: Reinforcement learning and local models on AMD Ryzen™ AI MAX+ with Unsloth
sortTitle: reinforcement learning and local models on amd ryzen™ ai max+ with unsloth
description:
Learn how to fine-tune AI models fully locally on AMD Ryzen™ AI MAX+ processors using Unsloth in this beginner to intermediate, instructor-led, hands-on workshop with no cloud required. Starting from dataset preparation and prompt formatting, you'll walk through the complete finetuning pipeline step by step. Along the way, you'll gain practical tips from industry experts and wrap up with a short challenge to put your freshly fine-tuned model to the test, all running on your own machine.

This Lab is BYOD. Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

title: Take LLMs from prototype to production on AKS
sortTitle: take llms from prototype to production on aks
description:
Moving an AI model from experiment to production is hard. Learn about AI Runway, an open-source accelerator that simplifies deploying LLMs on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS). By treating models as native Kubernetes resources, AI Runway offers a single interface that adapts to multiple inference backends. You’ll deploy a production LLM on AKS, implement custom resources for scaling and networking, configure GPU and latency monitoring, and integrate it into CI/CD pipelines.

Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

title: Terminal to Party: Live-Coding with GitHub Copilot CLI and Hydra
sortTitle: terminal to party: live-coding with github copilot cli and hydra
description:
Experience GitHub Copilot CLI while exploring OSS Hydra, a live-coding audio/visual environment that runs directly in the browser. Learn how to use agentic loops to create an AV party of your very own. EDM? Lo-fi? Have it all! You'll find your groove while exploring all that Copilot CLI has to offer, from planning, to managing multiple agents, to delegating longer tasks to the cloud. What better way to learn - and discover your new dev soundtrack! Warning. This program may contain flashing lights, strobe lights, loud noise, and/or smoke machines which may not be suitable for those with sensitivities.

Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

Experience GitHub Copilot CLI while exploring OSS Hydra, a live-coding audio/visual environment that runs directly in the browser. Learn how to use agentic loops to create an AV party of your very own. EDM? Lo-fi? Have it all! You'll find your groove while exploring all that Copilot CLI has to offer, from planning, to managing multiple agents, to delegating longer tasks to the cloud. What better way to learn - and discover your new dev soundtrack! Warning. This program may contain flashing lights, strobe lights, loud noise, and/or smoke machines which may not be suitable for those with sensitivities.

Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

sortTitle: any agent, any cloud: standardized tracing with foundry+opentelemetry

title: Beyond REST: .NET Apps That Talk to Hardware
sortTitle: beyond rest: .net apps that talk to hardware
description:
Most app demos stop at REST APIs. This live demo talks to real hardware. I’ll show a .NET app that discovers a device over BLE, exchanges data over NFC or serial, and turns raw device signals into a usable workflow. You’ll leave with a practical pattern for structuring device services, handling platform differences, and deciding when local connectivity beats the cloud.

title: Build context-aware agents using GitHub Copilot, Elastic, and Azure AI
sortTitle: build context-aware agents using github copilot, elastic, and azure ai
description:
Your AI reviewer has amnesia; it knows your code, but forgets the 2 AM outage from last month. We’ll demo giving Copilot a memory. Using Elastic Agent Builder and Workflows, we build an agent that reviews PRs against production telemetry and incident history. See it in action: an agent identifies a regression pattern, cites a past incident via OTel traces in Elasticsearch, and helps a dev fix it in VS Code via Copilot Agent Mode and the Elastic MCP server. Powered by Azure AI Foundry.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Build work-ready agents with Foundry + Work IQ, govern with Agent 365
sortTitle: build work-ready agents with foundry + work iq, govern with agent 365
description:
An agent that knows nothing about your work is only half an agent. In this demo, we connect a Foundry agent to Work IQ via MCP, giving it real context about your work and ecosystem, governed by Agent 365. See it run under agent identity, watch Work IQ tools invoked at runtime, and get full observability on every call. Then we run side-by-side evals context-aware vs. context-blind, with scores that prove the difference. Leave knowing how to ground, govern, and evaluate your Foundry agents.

title: Discovering Power App Data with M365 Copilot using MCP
sortTitle: discovering power app data with m365 copilot using mcp
description:

title: Multi-agents in action with 3 AI agents, 3 frameworks, tools & models
sortTitle: multi-agents in action with 3 ai agents, 3 frameworks, tools & models
description:
Three agent frameworks. One Agentic Startup Content Factory. Zero manual steps. In this live demo we build an intelligent system that researches topics, writes articles and code samples, and refines its own output, all while running autonomously. You'll walk out with an Agentic Startup Content Factory you can clone and ship.

title: OM1: Modular by Design, Hardware-Agnostic by Default. Your gateway to next-gen robotics
sortTitle: om1: modular by design, hardware-agnostic by default. your gateway to next-gen robotics
description: Robots today are powerful but siloed. Each one is locked to its own stack, hardware, and interfaces. OM1 is a modular, hardware-agnostic orchestration layer that bridges any robot with the cognitive infrastructure needed to make it actually useful. In this session, we'll walk through OM1's architecture, show how its modular design allows you to swap or add capabilities, and get started on your laptops. We'll also demo it live on the Unitree Go2 and in a cloud simulator. Bring a laptop if you'd like to follow along!

sortTitle: ship code faster with ai-powered nosql schema design
description:
NoSQL schema design is hard—denormalization decisions, partition key selection, and data modeling patterns require expertise. title: Smaller, faster, smarter: Distilling agents with fine‑tuning
sortTitle: smaller, faster, smarter: distilling agents with fine‑tuning
description:
Large models are powerful, but expensive to run in production. In this lightning talk, we’ll show how teams use Foundry for distillation and supervised fine-tuning to train small language models for task‑specific accuracy, dramatically reducing latency and cost. We’ll cover when distillation makes sense, how it complements fine tuning and reinforcement learning, and what real production teams have learned when deploying smaller models at scale. Expect fast examples and lots of Q&A.

title: Agentic FinOps: Governing Cost and Quality with Microsoft Foundry
sortTitle: agentic finops: governing cost and quality with microsoft foundry
description:

title: Agentic RAG for fast, low-cost knowledge retrieval on Azure Cosmos DB
sortTitle: agentic rag for fast, low-cost knowledge retrieval on azure cosmos db
description:
Skip the knowledge graph and ontology construction. DiverseRAG delivers high-quality answers to open-ended, multi-hop, and nuanced questions in seconds using GPU-accelerated local models and Azure Cosmos DB — combining hybrid full-text and vector search, diversity selection, and sub-question decomposition across large structured and unstructured datasets. You'll leave knowing how to build production-ready knowledge retrieval as long-term agentic memory to boost your agents' accuracy and speed.

title: Automate Windows on Arm migrations with AI-assisted specs
sortTitle: automate windows on arm migrations with ai-assisted specs
description:
Porting Windows apps to Arm used to take weeks: identifying dependencies, chasing compatibility gaps, and relying on trial and error. With AI, the same workflow now fits in a single session. This session shows Windows developers a spec-driven, AI-assisted workflow where a migration spec defines the architecture, GitHub Copilot applies Arm-aware changes, and GitHub Actions with WoA runners validate builds in CI. You’ll leave with a workflow that transforms your next migration from project to PR.

sortTitle: building your own mcp server

Modern applications demand elastic scale without complexity. In this session, we explore how Azure HorizonDBre-architects PostgreSQL for cloud-native scalability by decoupling compute, storage, and caching to deliver predictable performance at massive scale. See how it delivers sub millisecond latency, scales out reads and delivers 3x the transactional throughput of self-managed Postgres.

title: CopyFlag: Imagine Cup Finalists Tackling Creative Protection
sortTitle: copyflag: imagine cup finalists tackling creative protection
description:
CopyFlag helps independent creators fight AI-enabled infringement by continuously scanning marketplaces and social platforms for remixed copies. Built on Azure AI Vision, Azure OpenAI, Azure AI Search, and Azure Machine Learning, it vectorizes images, scores matches, and automates takedowns in minutes. Multi-model detection, combined with Azure AI Content Safety and offender network mapping, makes creator protection scalable and affordable.

title: Extend coding agents across your team with Claude in Microsoft Foundry
sortTitle: extend coding agents across your team with claude in microsoft foundry
description:
Many teams adopt a coding agent, then ask: why can't the rest of the org work this way? Claude Code handles engineering; Claude Cowork extends the same fundamentals to research, documents, and operational work across organizations. Live demos of both, pointed at work your teams already do.

title: Fire, forget, sleep. VS Code agents that run without you
sortTitle: fire, forget, sleep. vs code agents that run without you
description:
You can only be in one place at once. Your agents don't have that problem. Copilot agents in VS Code run three ways — local for interactive pairing, background for isolated tasks in worktrees, and cloud for autonomous long-running jobs. See when to reach for each, how to hand off between them, and manage parallel sessions from a single view. Custom agents, subagent orchestration, and delegating a local plan to background or cloud execution are all on the table.

title: From Code to Agents: Build Production MCP Servers on Azure Functions
sortTitle: from code to agents: build production mcp servers on azure functions
description:
Agents are transforming how we build software but taking them from prototype to production remains the biggest challenge enterprise teams face. AI Agents need to call real tools- and today that means fragile, custom integrations that break when anything changes. MCP gives agents a standard way to discover and invoke tools at runtime. See real-world scenarios such as a DevOps CI/CD automation system where agents identify detect deployment issues, diagnose, and create rollback PRs in Azure DevOps.

ogTitle: Make sure you understand the code AI is writing
ogDescription:
Copilot ships code fast. But neither you nor your agents understand what it does in production. That gap is growing. The old loop still looks like:
deploy → dashboards → logs → redeploy

It wasn’t built for AI-paced development. In this lightning talk, we break a real service in VS Code, then fix it using Copilot agent mode grounded in Dynatrace MCP. See runtime context flow into your editor so you and your agents can work in context and fix issues at the speed code is created.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: MCP does way more than you think
sortTitle: mcp does way more than you think
description:
You've connected an MCP server or two. But MCP in GitHub Copilot goes a lot further than tool lookups — and it works across the editor, CLI, and cloud. This session demos the range: connecting Copilot to databases, APIs, and internal systems wherever you work, plus a first look at MCP Apps, where tool calls return real UI like dashboards and forms right in chat. All live, all demos, no slides.

title: prompts.chat: Building an Open Prompt Ecosystem for the Age of Agents
sortTitle: prompts.chat: building an open prompt ecosystem for the age of agents
description:
Agents are getting more capable, but prompts still matter. The difference is prompts are no longer one-off inputs. They are becoming reusable building blocks for workflows, tools, and shared AI systems. I’ll share the story of prompts.chat and how it evolved from an open-source prompt library into a broader ecosystem, why prompts still matter in the age of agents, how they help define intent and context, and why open systems are important for collaboration, reuse, and better developer workflows.

title: Rubric-Based Evaluation for Real World AI Systems
sortTitle: rubric-based evaluation for real world ai systems
description:
AI teams need to prove their systems behave responsibly, but turning policies, safety requirements, or product expectations into evaluations is still slow and manual. This talk introduces a way to translate natural-language requirements into testable artifacts: concrete behaviors, targeted test cases, evaluation runs, and evidence of where the system aligns or fails. It helps teams move from “we have a requirement” to “we can measure whether the system follows it.

sortTitle: run ai coding agents on-device with npus
description:

sortTitle: solo agent to agent fleet: production multi-agent systems with dapr
description:

title: Stop vulnerabilities in AI-generated code before they ship
sortTitle: stop vulnerabilities in ai-generated code before they ship
description:
Apps ship in hours with Copilot and Cursor. Vibes are great, until the vulns show up. AI code introduces new risks: hallucinated packages and insecure patterns. This session breaks down Aikido's real-world research on why traditional AppSec fails 'vibe coding' and how attackers exploit this shift. We’ll cover AI vuln patterns, execution vectors, supply chain risks, and practical guardrails. Vibe coding is inevitable; shipping vulns isn’t. Walk away with a security blueprint.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Stop Writing Prompts. Start Writing Specs.
sortTitle: stop writing prompts. start writing specs.
description:
Prompt engineering got us started, but it often leads to fragile systems that are hard to maintain and do not inspire confidence. In this session, we explore a different path. Using GitHub Spec Kit we build an agent from a specification, treating the spec as the contract that defines capabilities, tools, and outputs. Instead of rewriting prompts, we evolve the system by evolving the spec.

title: What the shift from cloud apps to agentic systems means for developers
sortTitle: what the shift from cloud apps to agentic systems means for developers
description:
AI is changing how applications and agents are built and operated. This lightning talk explains how agentic systems reason over context, take multi‑step actions, and adapt over time—and what that means for developers. We’ll cover how design, building, delivery, and operations evolve as apps become agent‑driven, and how Microsoft’s integrated agentic platform is able to support apps and agents responsibly in production.

title: Why GitHub Copilot misses context (and how to fix it)
sortTitle: why github copilot misses context (and how to fix it)
description:
Out of the box, GitHub Copilot doesn't know your conventions, architecture opinions, or how you ship. But you can teach it. Set up shared rules and reusable prompts so Copilot follows your standards. Build custom agents and skills for workflows your team actually runs. Dial in settings that scale from five engineers to five hundred. Rules, skills, prompt files, custom agents — see how they fit and when to reach for each.

title: Adding AI to Real Apps: The Agentic Way
sortTitle: adding ai to real apps: the agentic way
description:
Most teams aren’t building AI apps from scratch—they’re adding AI to applications that already exist. And increasingly, they’re not writing that integration themselves. This Table Talk explores how agentic development workflows—Copilot CLI, agent mode, and cloud coding agents—are reshaping how AI capabilities get added to real apps. We’ll discuss practical integration patterns, where agents accelerate the work, and where they still need a human in the loop. Bring your own modernization stories as we compare what’s working, what isn’t, and where to start.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: AI Observability: What You Can’t See (and Why It Breaks)
sortTitle: ai observability: what you can’t see (and why it breaks)
description:
Traditional monitoring can’t catch LLM hallucinations or hidden token costs. In this discussion, we’ll move beyond "green" status lights to the architectural reality of running AI at scale. We will dive into Microsoft Foundry to explore correlating signals across infra, data, and model layers. Bring your production "war stories" as we debate how to use Azure AI Evaluation and tracing to build systems that are truly debuggable, measurable, and continuously improvable.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: AI safety at scale with the MAI team
sortTitle: ai safety at scale with the mai team
description:
Shipping AI safely is an ongoing process of experimentation, investigation, creativity, and design. We’ll share how we've built safety systems for large-scale nondeterministic systems – making them robust to emerging harms and adversarial actors, and how we tackle trade-offs like over-refusal. Expect a discussion-driven session on building AI that’s flexible, reliable, and accountable.

speakerNames: Nagkumar Arkalgud, Mikhail Simin, Hanchi Wang
title: Any agent, any cloud: Observability patterns that scale
sortTitle: any agent, any cloud: observability patterns that scale
description:
Agents ship across frameworks and clouds—but telemetry is often fragmented. Let’s map an observability plan: what to trace (model + tool calls), what to measure (latency, cost, quality), and how to optimize (using trace-linked insights). Bring your scenario; we’ll compare a few viable patterns, debate tradeoffs, and leave with a practical checklist of next actions for your stack.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Build & modernize multi-tenant AI app data planes for planetary scale
sortTitle: build & modernize multi-tenant ai app data planes for planetary scale
description:
Most AI-agent demos fail in production because their data platforms were never built for multi-tenant scale. This table talk connects two real journeys: modernizing relational systems to NoSQL, and running large-scale agentic SaaS. We’ll whiteboard migration tradeoffs, tenant-aware modeling, agent memory and retrieval patterns, plus hard lessons learned to ship reliable AI systems at scale today!

title: Building with AI on Systems You Don’t Fully Understand
sortTitle: building with ai on systems you don’t fully understand
description:
Most teams aren’t building on greenfield; they’re layering AI onto messy, poorly understood systems. What breaks when developers and AI agents lack visibility into system architecture and logic? In this discussion, we’ll compare approaches teams use today: reverse engineering, documentation methods, and queryable system intelligence. We’ll examine tradeoffs in speed, accuracy, and risk, and share ways to make systems more navigable for humans and AI so you can ship faster with fewer surprises.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Claws on Windows: Designing Safe, Bounded Agent Actions
sortTitle: claws on windows: designing safe, bounded agent actions
description:
Claws touch files, processes, networks, and user state. Poorly designed claws create security risk and brittle behavior. In this interactive table talk, two experts will break down real claw designs used today, including permission scoping, execution boundaries, observability, and rollback. Developers will compare approaches, challenge assumptions, and share what has failed in practice while shaping clearer guidance for building reliable claws on Windows.

title: Coding Agents in the Enterprise: What's Working? What's Not?
sortTitle: coding agents in the enterprise: what's working? what's not?
description:
With AI-assisted development on the rise, teams are rethinking how they build apps. They're using agents to write code, connect to data, provision services, and set access policies, all within existing compliance requirements. A common thread we're hearing: teams are struggling to unleash coding agents safely and maintainably. In this table talk, we'd love to hear where agents create the most value for your team and what keeps you up at night. We'll share learnings and best practices.

title: Designing AI Data Platforms on Azure Blob Beyond Retrieval
sortTitle: designing ai data platforms on azure blob beyond retrieval
description:
You have data in Azure Blob Storage. You add embeddings, connect to search, and ship. It works until you need to change the data itself. Curation, feature engineering, training, search, and analytics quickly spread data across multiple systems. This discussion explores how teams design AI data platforms on Azure beyond retrieval, comparing multi-system setups with approaches that treat Blob as the unified foundation for raw data, embeddings, and features, and the tradeoffs that come with each.

title: Designing AI Gateways: Routing, Cost, and Control
sortTitle: designing ai gateways: routing, cost, and control
description:
AI workloads don’t behave like traditional APIs—they span models, tools, and agents across providers, making routing, cost control, safety, and observability harder in production. In this table talk, we’ll explore how teams are designing AI gateway patterns, comparing approaches for multi-model routing, policy enforcement, and cost management. Join to discuss tradeoffs, production challenges, and what works when scaling AI systems.

AI is everywhere—but scaling it across organizations is still hard.

title: Fake It or Mask It: Choosing the Right Data for Agent Simulation Environments
sortTitle: fake it or mask it: choosing the right data for agent simulation environments
description:
Agents need realistic worlds to train in, but the data behind them is rarely safe to use raw. Two paths dominate: synthetic data that mirrors production, or de-identified real data that keeps its messiness without exposing real people. Each makes different tradeoffs on fidelity, edge cases, governance, and generalization. Let's compare notes on where each shine, where it breaks, and where teams blend the two.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: From Locked-In to Liquid: Modernizing .NET Before the November 2026 EOL
sortTitle: from locked-in to liquid: modernizing .net before the november 2026 eol
description:
.NET 8 and 9 both hit end of life on November 10, 2026. With hundreds of repos to upgrade, manual migration won't cut it—and pointing agents at the problem without structure burns tokens and produces inconsistent results. Let's talk about how agents, given the right deterministic tools, can move your .NET portfolio from rigid and locked-in to liquid—continuously modern, with swappable components and routine upgrades instead of fire drills. Bring your migration questions and stack horror stories.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

speakerNames: Nish Anil, Hazem El-Hammamy, Ryan Frazier
title: Modernizing at scale with agents: Patterns, pitfalls, and tradeoffs
sortTitle: modernizing at scale with agents: patterns, pitfalls, and tradeoffs
description: Agents promise to compress years of app modernization into quarters, but speed isn’t the only goal. Enterprises still need to prioritize the right apps, define agent autonomy, and balance velocity with risk and cost. In this discussion, we’ll examine how teams are sequencing portfolios, designing agent workflows, and enforcing guardrails, along with lessons on where agents deliver real value and where they don’t yet.

title: Multi-Agent Systems: When (and When Not) to Use Them
sortTitle: multi-agent systems: when (and when not) to use them
description:
Are multi-agent systems a breakthrough or architectural debt? While the hype is high, many teams over-engineer their AI stacks, leading to increased cost, latency, and complexity. This Table Talk focuses on when multi-agent architectures add real value and when simpler patterns are the better choice. We’ll explore trade-offs between RAG and multi-agent designs and discuss how to maintain control in production systems.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Multimodal data: Architecting pipelines that don’t break at scale
sortTitle: multimodal data: architecting pipelines that don’t break at scale
description:
Multimodal AI unlocks 90% of the world's data: video, audio, PDFs, sensor streams, etc. But processing it breaks traditional batch pipelines and leaves GPUs idle 50%+ of the time.

In this session, you'll learn:
-Why multimodal pipelines break traditional batch architectures
-How to eliminate CPU to GPU I/O bottlenecks
-How Anyscale built a streaming CPU+GPU engine with Ray Data
-How AV customers scale data-intensive workloads on Azure

For CV researchers, ML engineers, and physical AI teams.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Outcome-maxxing, not token-maxxing: what "good" looks like in AI-authored code
sortTitle: outcome-maxxing, not token-maxxing: what "good" looks like in ai-authored code
description:
AI is now the primary author in most codebases. Throughput is climbing. Bugs, incidents, churn, and review time are climbing faster. Developers are optimizing for tokens accepted, not code that survives. A working session, not a panel. We'll compare how teams redefine "good" in AI-authored code: where to set the bar at authorship vs. review, which guardrails earn their friction, and what to track when dashboards say faster but the team feels slower. Bring an argument you're tired of having.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

sortTitle: production mcp servers: auth, tools, and the patterns that hold up

Custom agents and platforms like Copilot are only as useful as the actions they can take in the systems that matter. title: Rethinking Retrieval with Foundry IQ
sortTitle: rethinking retrieval with foundry iq
description:
Most teams build retrieval the same way: custom pipelines on top of search. Foundry IQ aims to improve and changes that. This discussion explores traditional vs agentic retrieval, showing where accuracy improves and where latency tradeoffs appear. We’ll walk through real use cases, built-in multi-retrieval capabilities, and how Foundry IQ integrates with Azure AI Search and other IQ layers. The focus is on when to use it, what it replaces, and how to adopt it without rebuilding everything.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Scaling PostgreSQL for Agentic Apps: Patterns and Tradeoffs
sortTitle: scaling postgresql for agentic apps: patterns and tradeoffs
description:
Agentic apps place new demands on PostgreSQL - low-latency retrieval, the need to combine graph, transactional, and document models within a single system, integration with AI models. In this Table Talk, explore architecture patterns for agentic development with PostgreSQL using Azure Horizon DB. Compare approaches for embedding pipelines, agentic architectures - balancing performance, cost, and scale - and learn where designs break in production.

title: The Legacy Java Problem No One Wants to Inherit
sortTitle: the legacy java problem no one wants to inherit
description:
Every team has one: the Java app no one fully understands, running a runtime, no one wants to touch, with tests that may or may not exist. Modernizing it used to mean months of planning and a careful rewrite. Now AI agents are quietly changing the math. This Table Talk explores how teams are tackling legacy Java modernization with agentic tools—analyzing unfamiliar codebases, planning upgrades, and refactoring at a pace that wasn’t possible a year ago. We’ll trade stories on what’s working, what’s breaking, and how the role of the developer is shifting in the process.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Trusted AI Built for Production
sortTitle: trusted ai built for production
description:
Responsible AI is widely discussed, but hard to operationalize. Many teams struggle to move AI systems from MVP to production. This session covers defining risk thresholds (safety, jailbreaks, accuracy, hallucinations), generating test cases with LLMs, and building golden datasets. We’ll discuss evaluating multi-turn agentic systems using Azure evaluation SDKs and AI Red Teaming Agent, plus integration into CI/CD for repeatable, production-ready systems across prompts, models, and agents.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: What AI Can’t Replace: The Enduring Value of Human Judgment, Trust, and Connection
sortTitle: what ai can’t replace: the enduring value of human judgment, trust, and connection
description:
As AI agents take on more planning, execution, and coordination, human contribution is shifting—not disappearing. This session explores what remains uniquely human in an agentic world, and why those capabilities will increase in value.

We’ll examine where human judgment outperforms machine optimization, how trust is built in AI-mediated systems, and why connection and accountability can’t be fully virtualized.

Join the conversation to rethink the future of work, product design, and leadership—focusing on where human value matters most.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

sortTitle: why is debugging distributed, complex production systems still this hard?

You’ll implement a comment moderation workflow, compare model outputs, and package the solution as a hosted agent using Python, ready for real-world integration.

title: Digital Lab: Hands-on with the latest Cobalt VMs – from code to cloud deployment
sortTitle: digital lab: hands-on with the latest cobalt vms – from code to cloud deployment
description:
Roll up your sleeves and get hands-on with the latest Azure Cobalt VMs. Provision your first Cobalt VM and explore what makes Arm tick in the Cloud. title: Digital Lab: Improving agent behavior using reinforcement learning from traces
sortTitle: digital lab: improving agent behavior using reinforcement learning from traces
description:
AI agents often work, but their behavior can be inconsistent or hard to control with prompts alone. In this hands-on lab, you will start with a working agent, inspect execution traces, define “good behavior” using evaluation graders, and apply reinforcement learning to reinforce better decisions. By the end, you’ll have a practical workflow for improving agent behavior using feedback from agent runs, without redesigning your agent from scratch.

This is a self-paced, digitally-delivered lab experience that you will complete on your own schedule. Click the 'Launch' button to start the lab. NOTE:  This lab will be available to complete on June 2, 2026, starting at 12:00 PM Pacific Time.

title: Digital Lab: Take LLMs from prototype to production on AKS
sortTitle: digital lab: take llms from prototype to production on aks
description:
Moving an AI model from experiment to production is hard. Learn about AI Runway, an open-source accelerator that simplifies deploying LLMs on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS). By treating models as native Kubernetes resources, AI Runway offers a single interface that adapts to multiple inference backends. You’ll deploy a production LLM on AKS, implement custom resources for scaling and networking, configure GPU and latency monitoring, and integrate it into CI/CD pipelines.

This is a self-paced, digitally-delivered lab experience that you will complete on your own schedule. Click the 'Launch' button to start the lab. NOTE:  This lab will be available to complete on June 2, 2026, starting at 12:00 PM Pacific Time.

title: Agentic infrastructure needs agentic observability
sortTitle: agentic infrastructure needs agentic observability
description: Observability pipelines were built for a world where engineers manually standardized logs, traces, and metrics. That model is breaking down as AI generates services and infrastructure faster than pipelines can keep up. Explore a new model where observability layers reason over telemetry and adapt as systems evolve. Agent-driven workflows continuously interpret and normalize data, raising new questions around guardrails, validation, and autonomy.

title: Design systems for every user, including people and LLMs
sortTitle: design systems for every user, including people and llms
description: Accessibility is often viewed as a usability feature or compliance requirement, important but secondary. In AI‑driven systems, that no longer holds. LLMs interpret the content we produce, and their reliability depends on structure, semantics, and clarity. Accessible content is easier for humans and machines to understand, process, and reuse. This session reframes accessibility as a systems‑level engineering concern, showing why it outperforms OCR and leads to safer, more predictable AI outcomes.

deploy → dashboards → logs → redeploy
